channel.title: Chart Breakers & Star Stories
channel.itunes:author: Family TVR
channel.itunes:summary: Did your favorite artist just break a record or a heart? The charts are screaming, and we have the inside story you won't hear anywhere else.
channel.description: The latest music trends, chart updates, and star stories from Billboard and beyond. Stay updated with the Pop Culture.

item.title: Andrew Lloyd Webber Says He’s a Recovering Alcoholic
item.description:
[Intro music plays]
Host: Welcome to our podcast, where we dive into the latest news and trends in the world of entertainment. Today, we're shining the spotlight on legendary composer Andrew Lloyd Webber, who's opening up about his journey to sobriety.
[Soft music transitions in]
Host: In a recent interview with The Times, the 78-year-old EGOT winner shared that he's been on a path to recovery for over a year, after realizing he needed help with his addiction to alcohol. Lloyd Webber admitted that checking into a rehab clinic didn't work for him, but attending AA meetings has been a game-changer.
[Clip of Andrew Lloyd Webber's quote plays]: "I am a recovering alcoholic. Sixteen months ago I decided that I needed help and it's the best thing that ever happened to me."
Host: Lloyd Webber, who's behind iconic musicals like Cats, which is currently on Broadway, revealed that he'd started drinking again after previously quitting in 2015 and 2016. He shared that he was "white-knuckling" it, trying to quit on his own without any support, but ultimately started drinking secretly.
[Clip of Andrew Lloyd Webber's quote plays]: "I was doing what they call 'white-knuckling', without any backup, and I started to worry that I wasn't being creative."
Host: Thankfully, Lloyd Webber has found a new sense of creativity and support through his daily AA meetings. He's even made new friends and found a sense of community, which he says he never thought possible.
[Clip of Andrew Lloyd Webber's quote plays]: "People had always said, 'Oh no, you wouldn't like that.' And you get this thought that it's a load of meth drinkers coming in off the streets. Not at all. What I love about it is you go into a room and everybody's equal."
Host: As for his creative process, Lloyd Webber admitted that he may have written some of his hit songs while drinking, but he's now focusing on new projects, including two new musicals – one based on the film The Illusionist and the other on the theft of the Mona Lisa.
[Outro music plays]
Host: That's all for today's episode. Thank you for tuning in, and we'll catch you on the next one.
